channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: 10th Sunday of Pentecost
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: 10th Sunday of Pentecost

item.title: 10th Sunday of Pentecost, Healing Of The Lunatic Son, 2002
item.description: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. Healing Of The Lunatic Son, 2002
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. Healing Of The Lunatic Son, 2002

item.title: 10th Sunday of Pentecost, The Healing of the Demoniac Boy, The "Golden Chain" connecting faith and prayer and fasting., 2008-08-24
item.description: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The story of the healing of the demoniac boy illustrates two kinds of belief (faith) and two kinds of unbelief. If we want to have true and saving belief we must recognize the relationship between faith and prayer and fasting (and also understand that "fasting" involves much more than just abstaining from certain kinds of food).
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The story of the healing of the demoniac boy illustrates two kinds of belief (faith) and two kinds of unbelief. If we want to have true and saving belief we must recognize the relationship between faith and prayer and fasting (and also understand that "fasting" involves much more than just abstaining from certain kinds of food).

item.title: 10th Sunday of Pentecost, The Exorcism Of The Lunatic Son, Why fast?, 2009-08-16
item.description: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The Exorcism Of The Lunatic Son, Why fast?, 2009-08-16
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The Exorcism Of The Lunatic Son, Why fast?, 2009-08-16

item.title: 10th Sunday of Pentecost, Why could we not cast him out? , 2010-08-01
item.description: :10th Sunday of Pentecost., Why could we not cast him out? , 2010-08-01
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :10th Sunday of Pentecost., Why could we not cast him out? , 2010-08-01

item.title: 10th Sunday of Pentecost, Exorcism of the demoniac son., A very common question and the 2 ways to understand the answer., 2011-08-21
item.description: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The story of the exorcism of the lunatic son contains a very important question that all of us ask everyday - "Why?" We ask this question consciously and unconsciously and even dissatisfaction, confusion and ignorance are forms of this question. The solution is given in two parts. Firstly, our Lord tells us but we asked this question because of our unbelief, and the solution to this unbelief is given; prayer and fasting, and, very importantly, our admission of our unbelief and prayer that the Lord will help us to believe.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The story of the exorcism of the lunatic son contains a very important question that all of us ask everyday - "Why?" We ask this question consciously and unconsciously and even dissatisfaction, confusion and ignorance are forms of this question. The solution is given in two parts. Firstly, our Lord tells us but we asked this question because of our unbelief, and the solution to this unbelief is given; prayer and fasting, and, very importantly, our admission of our unbelief and prayer that the Lord will help us to believe.

item.title: 10th Sunday of Pentecost, 2012-08-11
item.description: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The words of St Paul regarding Apostles MUST also apply to us! If we want honor, apparent strength or wisdom, etc, we will not be followers of Christ. If we obey the apostle and are followers of him (we must understand this - we must also live in such a way that people follow us!), we will understand true prayer and virtue in all circumstances.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The words of St Paul regarding Apostles MUST also apply to us! If we want honor, apparent strength or wisdom, etc, we will not be followers of Christ. If we obey the apostle and are followers of him (we must understand this - we must also live in such a way that people follow us!), we will understand true prayer and virtue in all circumstances.

item.title: 10th Sunday of Pentecost, The exorcism of the lunatic son., 2012-08-12
item.description: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The exorcism of the lunatic son teaches us how to gain faith. There is no faith, no salvation without labor. We must understand prayer and fasting in this context. We also see that our sins CAN and DO harm others.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The exorcism of the lunatic son teaches us how to gain faith. There is no faith, no salvation without labor. We must understand prayer and fasting in this context. We also see that our sins CAN and DO harm others.

item.title: 10th Sunday of Pentecost, 2013-09-01
item.description: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The disciples were massively successful when they went out two by two, and then the encountered a boy from who they could not cast out a demon. This perplexed and worried them, and they asked the Lord "Why could not we cast him out?" The answer, recorded precisely because it applies to all of us, is it was because of their unbelief. how does belief vs. unbelief manifest in a Christian who encounters a problem? We do not have far to go, the Lord promised that we only need a little belief, and He will magnify it: "If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain..." Let us talk about how to do this small thing. You can do this.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. The disciples were massively successful when they went out two by two, and then the encountered a boy from who they could not cast out a demon. This perplexed and worried them, and they asked the Lord "Why could not we cast him out?" The answer, recorded precisely because it applies to all of us, is it was because of their unbelief. how does belief vs. unbelief manifest in a Christian who encounters a problem? We do not have far to go, the Lord promised that we only need a little belief, and He will magnify it: "If ye have faith as a grain of mustard seed, ye shall say unto this mountain..." Let us talk about how to do this small thing. You can do this.

item.title: 10th Sunday of Pentecost, 2014-08-17
item.description: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. Because we are made in the image of God, we have the privilege to do Godly things, such as being creators of life. This is parenthood, and carries with it a great responsibility. We look at the words of St Paul in the light of Christian parenthood: " For though ye have ten thousand instructors in Christ, yet have ye not many fathers: for in Christ Jesus I have begotten you through the gospel. Wherefore I beseech you, be ye followers of me."
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :10th Sunday of Pentecost. Because we are made in the image of God, we have the privilege to do Godly things, such as being creators of life. This is parenthood, and carries with it a great responsibility. We look at the words of St Paul in the light of Christian parenthood: " For though ye have ten thousand instructors in Christ, yet have ye not many fathers: for in Christ Jesus I have begotten you through the gospel. Wherefore I beseech you, be ye followers of me."
